They mention that even though basic needs like food and warmth are essential, once those are met, people need to figure out their existence. The key part here is where the author states, "They believe that man cannot live by bread alone... something else... which everyone needs, and that is to figure out who we are and why we are here." B might be a true statement, but the question is about what the author believes, and the main thesis is more about the universal philosophical questions. So B might not be the best answer. C) Everyone has to figure out who we are and why we are here. This directly matches the statement in the text where the author says that everyone needs to figure out their existence.
